surface.name: Experience Platform API authentication tutorial and XDM classes credential test
surface.selection_basis: Adobe's official Authenticate and access Experience Platform APIs tutorial explicitly promises the three headers required for Platform calls, orders the one-time access and project setup, generates an OAuth Server-to-Server token, and ends by testing the credentials with a standard XDM classes GET. It is the clearest platform-wide API first success, though product-specific workflows and the newer API-reference credential UI are co-equal alternatives.

alternatives_considered.name: Credentials directly in API reference
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Adobe documents this newer shortcut, but the main tutorial still explains the complete Developer Console integration and permission model.

alternatives_considered.name: Adobe Postman environments and collections
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The credential test itself uses Postman, but Adobe's optional downloadable environments and collections add broader reusable setup beyond importing the one displayed cURL request.

alternatives_considered.name: Deprecated Service Account JWT
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Adobe says JWT is deprecated and OAuth Server-to-Server is the only supported method for new integrations.

alternatives_considered.name: Product-specific Experience Platform APIs
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Schema, Catalog, Ingestion, Segmentation, Destinations, Privacy, and other APIs add different sandbox, permission, payload, and terminal outcomes.

entry_point.developer_intent: Obtain authorized Adobe Experience Platform API credentials and prove them by listing the standard XDM classes available to the organization.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Adobe Experience Platform APIs.
The organization is licensed for Experience Platform, but the developer's Adobe ID, product-profile assignments, project, credentials, and token have not been established.
An Admin Console administrator is available for the developer and user product-profile grants required before integration creation.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer is added as both developer and user for an Experience Platform product profile, creates an Adobe Developer Console project with Experience Platform API and OAuth Server-to-Server credentials, generates an access token, imports Adobe's displayed global Schema Registry classes cURL request into Postman, executes it, and receives standard XDM classes.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: GET https://platform.adobe.io/data/foundation/schemaregistry/global/classes returns a results array containing entries such as XDM ExperienceEvent and XDM Individual Profile, after which the guide says the credentials are valid and working.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The tutorial's Test access credentials section says a response similar to its XDM classes example means the credentials are valid and working; Next steps says the credentials were gathered and successfully tested.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It is Adobe's explicit end of the authentication tutorial and proves enterprise entitlement, administrator assignments, Developer Console integration, API key, organization ID, OAuth token, and a real Platform API response. Product-specific resource work follows later.

prerequisites.requirement: An organization with access to Adobe Experience Platform is required.
An Adobe ID is required; a developer without one must use Create a new account in Adobe Developer Console and complete signup.
An Admin Console administrator must add the developer as both a developer and a user for an Experience Platform product profile.

An Experience Platform system administrator is required later to grant attribute-based access controls and assign API credentials to a role for protected resource operations, but Adobe explicitly says the global classes call is sufficient to test credentials before that grant.
A browser and Postman are needed for the tutorial's literal credential test, which says to import and execute the displayed cURL request in Postman.
No card-entry step is documented, but the organization-level Experience Platform entitlement is a prerequisite.

primary_path.action: Sign in to Adobe Developer Console with an Adobe ID or select Create a new account and complete signup.
primary_path.output: Adobe ID and Developer Console session
primary_path.success_signal: The correct organization's Developer Console is accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The tutorial does not enumerate Adobe ID signup, verification, organization invitation, or enterprise identity-provider screens.

primary_path.action: Add the developer to an Experience Platform product profile as a developer and as a user.
details: In Admin Console, choose Products, Adobe Experience Platform, the relevant profile such as AEP-Default-All-Users, Developers, Add Developer, enter email or username, and Save.
User access is separately required to see UI outcomes.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer appears in the profile and can create Platform integrations.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing administrator authority or unprovisioned product access blocks the integration and requires organizational action.

primary_path.action: Create an empty project in Adobe Developer Console, using the organization selector to confirm the intended organization.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong organization or missing developer access prevents the needed API from being available.

primary_path.action: Select Add API, choose Adobe Experience Platform, select Experience Platform API, and select Next.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The API is absent when entitlement or product-profile developer access is missing.

primary_path.action: Choose OAuth Server-to-Server, enter a meaningful credential name, and select Next.
details: Adobe says this is the only token-generation method supported for new integrations.
Do not choose the deprecated JWT route.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Using legacy JWT conflicts with the documented new-integration path.

primary_path.action: Select AEP-Default-All-Users and any additional required product profiles, then select Save configured API.
primary_path.success_signal: The OAuth Server-to-Server project page shows Client ID and Organization ID.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting an insufficient profile or lacking profile access can later produce authorization failures.

primary_path.action: Copy API_KEY (Client ID) and ORG_ID, select Generate access token, and copy ACCESS_TOKEN.
details: Client ID and Organization ID are reusable.
The access token must be regenerated every 24 hours.
primary_path.output: API key, organization ID, and access token
primary_path.success_signal: All three required header values are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing product profile, invalid credential, or expired token blocks API calls.

primary_path.action: Copy Adobe's displayed cURL request for GET https://platform.adobe.io/data/foundation/schemaregistry/global/classes, import it into Postman, substitute ACCESS_TOKEN, API_KEY, and ORG_ID, and execute it.
details: The request uses Accept: application/vnd.adobe.xed-id+json, Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}, x-api-key: {{API_KEY}}, and x-gw-ims-org-id: {{ORG_ID}}.
The global classes test does not show a sandbox-name header, though many other Platform APIs require one.
Adobe's ordered instruction is to import and execute this cURL artifact in Postman; direct terminal execution is a technically possible branch, not the literal primary tool journey.
primary_path.input: Displayed cURL request, Postman, ACCESS_TOKEN, API_KEY, and ORG_ID
primary_path.output: Schema Registry XDM classes response in Postman
primary_path.success_signal: Postman receives a results array rather than an authentication error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Expired token, wrong organization, API-key mismatch, missing profile, malformed import, or unreplaced placeholders requires correcting the credential or Postman request and retrying.

primary_path.action: Verify the Postman response resembles Adobe's example and includes standard XDM class entries.
details: The example includes XDM ExperienceEvent and XDM Individual Profile with ids and versions.
Adobe says this response means the credentials are valid and working, while separately warning that protected resources require later attribute-based access-control permissions.
primary_path.output: Successfully tested Experience Platform API credentials
primary_path.success_signal: The expected standard XDM class objects appear in results.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An empty, malformed, authentication-error, or other error response does not satisfy the tutorial's credential-valid boundary.

branches.condition: The developer has no Adobe ID.
branches.path: Open Adobe Developer Console, select Create a new account, complete signup and any verification, then sign in and confirm the organization.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds identity creation before enterprise access can be assigned.

branches.condition: The developer or user profile assignment is missing.
branches.path: Have an Admin Console administrator add the identity to both roles in the Experience Platform product profile, wait for access to propagate, sign in again, and retry project setup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds administrator coordination and a potential propagation wait.

branches.condition: The API-reference credential shortcut is preferred.
branches.path: Open an Experience Platform API reference page, select Sign in, choose Personal or Company or School Account, select Create new credential, use the credentials dropdown to generate a token and copy API key and organization ID, then populate Try it or the cURL test.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Bypasses the separate Developer Console project screens but still depends on enterprise permissions.

branches.condition: The access token is older than 24 hours or rejected as expired.
branches.path: Return to the OAuth Server-to-Server credential page, select Generate access token, copy the new value, replace the Bearer token, and resend the request.

branches.condition: The developer chooses to execute the displayed cURL artifact directly in a terminal instead of following Adobe's Postman instruction.
branches.path: Replace ACCESS_TOKEN, API_KEY, and ORG_ID in the displayed request, execute that same GET and four headers with cURL, and inspect the JSON response. Treat this as a tool substitution using Adobe's artifact, not as the ordered primary interface documented by the tutorial.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes the Postman import while preserving the same endpoint, headers, and terminal signal.

branches.condition: After credential success, the developer calls a protected Experience Platform resource.
branches.path: Have an Experience Platform system administrator grant the needed attribute-based permissions and assign the API credential to the appropriate role, wait for access, then call the protected resource.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Does not alter the completed global-classes credential test; it adds a later administrator gate for protected operations.

branches.condition: The developer adopts Adobe's reusable Postman environments and collections after the one-request test.
branches.path: Download and import the Experience Platform Postman environment and Identity Management Service or product collection, populate or generate credentials, select the environment, and use collection requests.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds reusable Postman setup after the single imported cURL request already proved the credentials.

branches.condition: A later API requires a sandbox header.
branches.path: Determine a valid sandbox available to the organization and add x-sandbox-name with that value before sending the product-specific request.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds sandbox discovery and selection beyond the global classes credential test.

friction_gates.description: An Adobe ID and licensed Experience Platform organization are required.
An Admin Console administrator must grant both developer and user product-profile access.
Developers with multiple organizations must select the correct enterprise organization.
Product-profile selection determines what the integration can access.
Three headers are required, and the OAuth access token expires every 24 hours.
The literal test journey requires Postman so the displayed cURL request can be imported and executed as Adobe directs.

sources.title: Authenticate and access Experience Platform APIs
sections_used: Generate an API key and organization ID
Add Experience Platform to a project
evidence_supported: Complete account, administrator, project, credential, token, execution, verification, expiration, and alternative-route journey

sources.title: Sandboxes overview
evidence_supported: Organization sandbox partitions and later x-sandbox-name branch

uncertainties.question: Which Experience Platform API and business workflow should represent the workshop after generic authentication?
uncertainties.impact: Schema, ingestion, catalog, profile, segmentation, destinations, privacy, and other APIs add different roles, sandbox headers, payloads, and success signals.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The authentication tutorial intentionally stops at a generic global XDM classes credential test.

uncertainties.question: What exact enterprise procurement, organization invitation, and identity-provider journey precedes Admin Console access?
uncertainties.impact: This can add contract, administrator, SSO, invitation, and waiting gates before the documented developer steps.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The tutorial assumes an organization already has Experience Platform and begins with administrator assignments.

uncertainties.question: Should the workshop use the full Developer Console project route or the newer API-reference credential shortcut?
uncertainties.impact: The shortcut removes several pages, but the full route teaches the reusable project and permission model.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Adobe documents both as valid and does not declare one universally preferred.

excluded_after_success: Purchasing or provisioning the Experience Platform organization
Product-specific schemas, datasets, ingestion, profiles, segments, destinations, queries, and privacy workflows
Attribute-based access-control grants and API-role assignment for protected resource operations after the global classes credential test
Sandbox creation or administration beyond noting later header requirements
Production credential rotation, secret storage, token automation, retries, and monitoring
Reusable Postman environment and collection setup beyond importing the one displayed credential-test request
